data.title: Gray Media to buy Block Communications' TV stations for $80 million, enhancing presence in Midwest markets.
data.description: On August 1, 2025, Gray Media, Inc. announced the acquisition of Block Communications, Inc.'s television stations for $80 million. The deal aims to bolster Gray's Midwest influence through the establishment of a Big Four duopoly in one market. Additionally, Gray will gain top-performing local news stations in two other midwestern markets. This move is projected to enhance Gray's existing strong local stations in neighboring markets, expanding its overall reach and impact in the region.

data.title: VeChain (VET) remains at $0.02 post Franklin Templeton inclusion. RSI at 43.43 while MACD indicates bearish trend.
data.description: Following the integration of the Franklin Templeton fund, the price of VET has stabilized at $0.02. Technical analysis reveals a neutral RSI of 43.43, indicating no clear overbought or oversold conditions. The bearish MACD momentum hints at potential consolidation in the near future.

data.title: ATI Physical Therapy transitions from public to private ownership for long-term growth and operational flexibility.
data.description: ATI Physical Therapy, located in Downers Grove, Illinois, has recently transitioned from being publicly traded to privately owned as of August 1, 2025. The move from public to private ownership is aimed at supporting the long-term growth of the company, enhancing operational flexibility, and maintaining a strong focus on providing excellent patient care. Previously trading under the symbol ATIP on the OTC Markets, ATI Physical Therapy's shift in ownership reflects a strategic decision to prioritize sustained development and dedicated patient services.

data.title: Western Funding launches first-ever asset-backed securitization with $400 million issuance, marked by 4 note classes.
data.description: Western Funding, Inc. (WFIM), a subsidiary of Westlake Technology Holdings, achieved a significant milestone by successfully completing its first asset-backed securitization (ABS), WEFUN 2025-1, worth $400 million. The ABS consisted of four note classes: A worth $206,627,000 with an average life of 2.35 years and an interest rate of 4.75%, B worth $75,000,000 with an average life of 2.89 years and an interest rate of 4.98%, C worth $82,500,000 with an average life of 3.26 years and an interest rate of 5.34%, and D worth $35,873,000 with an average life of 3.46 years and an interest rate of 5.79%. This accomplishment represents a significant step in Western Funding's corporate development.

data.title: Ocean Bank's assets reach $7.25 billion, loans at $5.69 billion, and deposits at $6.13 billion, showing continued growth. President expresses gratitude to the team.
data.description: In Miami, it was reported by Ocean Bank that as of the second quarter ending on June 30, total assets have surged to $7.25 billion, showcasing an 8 percent rise from the prior year's end and a 13 percent increase from the second quarter of 2024. The total loans also saw an uptrend, reaching $5.69 billion, and deposits climbed to $6.13 billion as of June 30. Ocean Bank's President Chairman and CEO, Agostinho Alfonso Macedo, expressed gratitude and pride towards the Ocean Bank team for their efforts that led the bank to surpass the $7 billion asset milestone.
